prompt: Set up proper error handling with thiserror and anyhow

turns.content:
<task>
Set up proper error handling. Currently we're using unwrap() everywhere and the app panics on any error.
</task>

arguments.query: thiserror anyhow Rust error handling best practices library vs application

turns.content:
1. thiserror - Rust - https://docs.rs/thiserror/latest/thiserror/
   Derive macro for custom error types. Use in libraries.
2. anyhow - Rust - https://docs.rs/anyhow/latest/anyhow/
   Flexible error handling for applications. anyhow::Result, context(), bail!.
3. Error handling in Rust - https://nick.groenen.me/posts/rust-error-handling/
   Use thiserror for library error types, anyhow for application-level error propagation.

arguments.title: Rust error handling: thiserror + anyhow
arguments.content:
thiserror: derive Error for custom types. #[error("msg {field}")] for display. #[from] for automatic From impl.
anyhow: application-level. anyhow::Result<T>, .context("msg")? for adding context, bail!("msg") for early return.
Pattern: thiserror for library error enums, anyhow in main/CLI/application code.
Replace .unwrap() with ? operator.

turns.content: Finding recorded: Rust error handling: thiserror + anyhow

arguments.summary: Will add thiserror for custom ScraperError enum and use ? operator instead of unwrap(). anyhow for main().

next_steps.description: Add thiserror and anyhow to Cargo.toml
Create src/error.rs with ScraperError enum using thiserror
Replace unwrap() calls with ? operator in scraper.rs

turns.content:
1	 [package]
2	 name = "web-scraper"
3	 version = "0.1.0"
4	 edition = "2021"
5	 
6	 [dependencies]
7	 reqwest = { version = "0.11", features = ["blocking"] }
8	 scraper = "0.19"

arguments.content:
[dependencies]
reqwest = { version = "0.11", features = ["blocking"] }
scraper = "0.19"
thiserror = "1"
anyhow = "1"

arguments.message: Replaced all unwrap() calls with proper error handling. Functions now return Result types. ScraperError provides specific error variants for HTTP and selector errors. The app will report errors instead of panicking.

arguments.summary: Set up error handling with thiserror (ScraperError enum) and replaced all unwrap() with ? operator. Functions return Result types. Compiles cleanly.
arguments.final_answer: Error handling set up: ScraperError enum with thiserror derive, Http and Selector variants. All unwrap() replaced with ? operator. Functions return Result. Use anyhow::Result in main() for easy propagation.
